name: Ginger-Meatball and Watercress Soup
recipeIngredient: 4 1/2 tsp peeled and minced fresh ginger
recipeInstructions:
1. Finely chop 12 of the reserved water chestnuts.
2. Combine pork, ginger, chopped water chestnuts, soy sauce, cornstarch, salt, and pepper in a bowl. Mix well and form into balls about 3/4 inch in diameter. These can be made ahead and frozen; thaw completely in the refrigerator before poaching.
3. Bring vegetable stock and chicken stock to a simmer in a large pot. Add a portion of meatballs to the broth and gently poach until they float to the surface. Remove and keep warm. Repeat with remaining meatballs.
4. About 20 minutes before serving, return stocks to a boil and add reserved meatballs. Cook for 10-15 minutes (meatballs will remain slightly pink in center even after second cooking). Season with salt and pepper to taste.
5. Turn heat to medium low. Add watercress and green onions. Cook uncovered until watercress is slightly wilted and bright green, about a few minutes. Add reserved water chestnuts and cook for 1 minute to ensure they remain crisp.
6. Serve immediately.
Serve as the first course of a traditional Chinese dinner or alone as a light nutritious supper.
